compute.cost_note: stdlib + Tier 1-3 variance signals; CPU only, no model loads, no API costs (the DIPPER paraphraser is the gated M2 seam)

do_not_use_when: You want a single robustness or accuracy number — this surface refuses any aggregate score
You want a provenance verdict for a document — it is not a detector
You want proof a signal is paraphrase-robust — a flat proxy curve is never that (a stronger paraphraser approaches the 0.5-AUROC overlap floor)

examples.description: Regenerate a 3-pass stdlib-proxy ladder from one input

optional: --build-proxy IN.txt --passes N to regenerate a stdlib-proxy ladder fixture
inputs.target: fixture directory containing a ladder.jsonl manifest of {id, paraphraser, rungs} ladders (rung 0 is the clean base)

purpose: Walk a recursive-paraphrase ladder (a text paraphrased again and again) and emit a per-(signal × rung) decay curve for SETEC's existing signals: the per-rung relative change plus a per-cell stability label. Reuses the validation harness's audit_text + classify_compression scoring and adversarial_robustness_card's per-cell shape (the card's aggregate dict is never embedded). The M1 paraphraser is a deterministic stdlib proxy (proxy_stdlib); a realistic DIPPER ladder is the gated M2 seam. Not a detector; emits no aggregate robustness or accuracy score, and its ClaimLicense refuses any "robust to paraphrase" claim by quoting Sadasivan's separability ceiling (arXiv:2303.11156).
use_when: You have a graded ladder of paraphrase passes (an editor's successive passes, a humanizer tool's output, or the bundled stdlib proxy) and want the per-signal decay curve across rungs
You're prioritizing which SETEC signals erode fastest under recursive paraphrase
You want an honesty-claim artifact ("signal S shows relative change Δ at rung i") framed against the human/AI separability ceiling, not a robustness leaderboard
